rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly frames itself as extending prior work on reactions to other brand users and self-brand connection, and the cited paper is a close prior study on observers’ reactions to conspicuous brand users moderated by self-brand connection. The construct overlap is strong (self-brand connection, user-driven threat/dilution responses), making this plausibly a substantive theoretical/mechanism source rather than an incidental mention. The DOI match confirms the specific cited article (10.1509/jmr.11.0342).
